title: Control Stack Report FAIL: comparison_table seismology row claims orbit_path ending in satellite
_fixture_purpose: FAIL fixture: structurally well-formed but the comparison_table seismology row claims orbit_path=[singularity,satellite,satellite] and path_length_k=3 instead of [singularity,satellite,cosmos] and path_length_k=2. Two separate errors: wrong orbit path (COMPARISON_TABLE_MISMATCH — orbit_paths not uniform) and wrong path_length_k (COMPARISON_TABLE_MISMATCH — path lengths not uniform). Validator recomputes cross-row consistency from the table and detects both.
theorem_statement: The QA plan-control compiler law is domain-generic: orbit trajectory and path_length_k are preserved across distinct physical domains.

domain_summaries.one_line: The kernel-governed compilation relation preserving orbit trajectory and path length.
flat→stripes→hexagons via increase_amplitude + set_frequency; orbit singularity→satellite→cosmos; k=2.

comparison_table.notes: INCORRECTLY declared: orbit_path ends in satellite (should be cosmos); path_length_k=3 (should be 2). The report misrepresents the machine-verified seismic control cert.

canonical_pass_witness.description: Both domains share orbit_path=[singularity,satellite,cosmos] and path_length_k=2.

canonical_fail_witness.description: Seismology trace misrepresents orbit and path length.

validation_checks.description: all comparison_table rows have consistent orbit_path
all comparison_table rows have equal path_length_k

fail_ledger.message: comparison_table rows are not cross-domain consistent: orbit_paths differ (cymatics ends cosmos, seismology ends satellite) and path_length_k differs (2 vs 3). The report inaccurately represents the machine-verified control stack results.
